100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 1, 1926, a general strike began in Britain, fueled by widespread discontent among coal miners and workers demanding better wages and working conditions. The strike quickly escalated, affecting various industries and bringing much of the country to a standstill. Meanwhile, in the United States, President Calvin Coolidge addressed the nation, emphasizing economic stability and growth amidst rising labor tensions. Globally, tensions simmered in Germany as political unrest continued to challenge the Weimar Republic's stability.
Updated: Friday, May 1, 2026 at 07:45:33 AM CDT
